question.text: Under Section 23 of the Companies Act, 2013, a public company may issue securities through which of the following methods? (i) Public offer via prospectus (ii) Private placement (iii) Rights issue or bonus issue (iv) Preferential allotment to promoters only

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Section 23 of the Companies Act, 2013 permits a public company to issue securities through: (a) public offer via prospectus, (b) private placement, and (c) rights issue or bonus issue. "Preferential allotment to promoters only" is not a recognized statutory method under Section 23. **The Trap:** Preferential allotment exists under SEBI ICDR Regulations, but it is not limited to promoters and is not listed as a separate method under Section 23. Candidates may confuse ICDR provisions with the Companies Act framework. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series IX and Series XII both test the Companies Act framework for issue of securities. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Section 23 is the gateway provision — always start here to determine which issue route applies before diving into SEBI-specific regulations.
